author: Hexy Nodes
msg:
I’m hoping the team can help me understand an issue I’m experiencing. I am legitimately running 7 devices on my home Wi-Fi network, but I keep encountering a VPN error message, even though I am not using any VPN service. After reaching out to support, I was informed that my IP address has been banned.

Could you please explain how the team detects and flags an IP address as using a VPN? I’m concerned that this system may occasionally affect legitimate users who have no VPN activity and limited ways to contact support, resulting in them being unintentionally disconnected from the network. How many of those 10k VPN users were legitimate?

Thank you for your assistance.

author: Jack | Will Never Message You First
msg: [Media] Hi there, this process is still being calibrated to ensure outliers like this are avoided. Please submit your feedback to: https://forms.gle/womUzD5u2fbEGbbHA

author: Hexy Nodes

author: Sophie | I will never DM you first
msg: All tasks on the Unity Network carry reward potential - there are no “free” tasks. Every task on the network is driven by commercial demand from customers who pay for the service. The reward you receive depends on the task type, your device, your network setup and task availability. 60+ task types are planned across the network’s roadmap, each serving different customer needs and valuing different setups differently.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

author: ExcitedBlowfish 🐡
msg:
Rewards are based on what customers will pay, and it can change from hour to hour and region.

to put it simple, a customer can ask for a very specific set of telemetry in specific regions and timeframes.

The job for Unity is to provide consistent high quality data, and pay operators (UNO/ULO)

This is very simplified explanation leaving out details.

author: VeganCatLover

author: Wopdiddly
msg:
I believe it but I just don't want to wait, but, it is what it is and I have seen enough to suggest it'll be worth the wait.

author: Wopdiddly
msg: That makes sense, a telecom data marketplace

author: ExcitedBlowfish 🐡
msg:
that's actually what the exchange they are building will be.

S&R as I understand it are more specialised or targeted tasks.

The reason Unity is unique, is due to the broad variety of devices, locations, carriers etc.

No one can come close to this.
Some carriers globally have people hired to sort of mimic this, but they are limited in time and logistics.

Anyone can spin up synthetic devices in a datacenter, but customers are not really interested in that (some are, but not the majority).

msg: That all makes perfect sense and well communicated
